methodsAttempted.query: site:charlotte.edu OR site:uncc.edu "generative AI" "UNC Charlotte" policy students faculty ChatGPT academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Student Accountability, Center for Teaching and Learning, Legal Affairs, OneIT, and Inside UNC Charlotte AI pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:oneit.charlotte.edu "AI Software Guidance" "UNC Charlotte" approved rating generative AI data privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found OneIT AI Software Guidance and Security Checklist pages with data handling, tool rating, procurement, and security language.
Official-domain follow-up confirmed the strongest AI-specific sources were on accountability.charlotte.edu, teaching.charlotte.edu, and oneit.charlotte.edu.
Student Accountability links to Legal Affairs suggested syllabus language, OneIT AI software guidance, and Code of Student Academic Integrity context.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found current UNC Charlotte AI-specific guidance from Student Accountability & Conflict Resolution, OneIT, and the Center for Teaching and Learning. The strongest claim evidence covers instructor communication around AI use, disclosure-oriented academic integrity sample language, OneIT software ratings and procurement review, AI detection/grading tool treatment, and AI data security. An Inside UNC Charlotte news article was official but rejected as news/context rather than current policy or guidance evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Syllabus Guidelines on the Use of AI Tools

stopConditions: Stop after official AI-specific pages support direct claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide binding policy from sample syllabus language or news articles.
Preserve the CTL page caveat that its examples do not reflect institution-level or department-level AI policy.

artifacts.claimText: UNC Charlotte Student Accountability & Conflict Resolution recommends that faculty clearly communicate what AI use is permitted in courses, academic exercises, or assessments, and identifies written notice about how and when AI tools may be used as a best practice.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Faculty best practice is written communication of permitted AI use.

artifacts.claimText: UNC Charlotte Student Accountability & Conflict Resolution provides assignment and exam sample language stating that, where AI use is permitted, transparency is required and failure to disclose AI use may be considered a Code of Student Academic Integrity violation.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Sample assignment language treats undisclosed AI use as a possible academic integrity violation when disclosure is required.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: To support academic integrity and meaningful learning, faculty should clearly communicate what kind of AI use is permitted in their courses or in particular academic exercises or assessments. ... As a best practice, faculty members should notify students, in writing, about how and when AI tools (such as ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, etc.) may be used in their assignments, exams, and other course work.

artifacts.notes: Approved as non-binding teaching guidance because the source says best practice and should, not must.
Approved narrowly as sample assignment/exam language, not as a universal AI disclosure requirement for all courses.
Approved as OneIT procurement/request guidance for non-campus-wide AI software.
Approved from the OneIT software table; claim is scoped to listed ratings and does not assert a complete tool inventory beyond the page.
Approved as AI-specific data handling guidance from OneIT.
Approved as source-status caveat to prevent overstatement of CTL course-level examples as institution-level policy.

artifacts.title: University of North Carolina at Charlotte AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: UNC Charlotte has official AI-specific public guidance from Student Accountability & Conflict Resolution and OneIT. Student Accountability recommends written faculty communication about permitted AI use and provides sample disclosure language tied to academic integrity. OneIT provides AI software ratings, non-campus-wide software request guidance, and security guidance against entering confidential, legally restricted, or Level 3 data into AI tools. The Center for Teaching and Learning AI syllabus page was retained as a source-status caveat because it says its course-level examples do not provide or use a university-created AI policy and do not reflect institution-level or department-level AI policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Sample syllabus and assignment language is staged as guidance/source context only and should not be promoted as a universal requirement unless reviewed against an official binding policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
